claim: Sophia's source-discipline pipeline (sophia_full) reduces false attribution and contested fabrication versus the raw base model, at bounded false-positive cost, on the held-out external-labeled SEIB-100 provenance set.
scorer: deterministic_seib_scorer; labels are external (provenance_bench external-citation / Wikidata snapshot); the scorer is independent of agent.verifiers, and the gate is treatment only (non-circular).
falsification: raw model matches or beats sophia_full on provenance accuracy (raw_to_full_accuracy_delta <= 0, or its 95% bootstrap CI includes 0)
sophia_full asserts any false attribution (falseAttributionRate > 0) or fabricates on any contested case (fabricationRateOnContested > 0)
the gate erases correct gold attributions beyond tolerance (falsePositiveCost > 0.10)
any eval prompt is found in the training corpus (training overlap > 0)
the eval pack sha256 does not match the value recorded here
the only evidence is a single run or a mock run
claimBoundary: A PASS confirms the gate's provenance uplift ON THIS PACK ONLY, recomputed live by a third party. It is NOT an AGI claim, NOT external generalization beyond this pack, and NOT a hallucination guarantee. A FAIL refutes the uplift claim on this pack.
immutabilityNote: Thresholds live in this version-controlled file, not in the runner. tools/run_external_validation.py prints this file's sha256 at run time so a reviewer can confirm the registration predates the result it judges, and recomputes the verdict from the live per-case rows rather than trusting any committed result artifact.
